title: COVID and the Coup
summary: Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux answer questions about security, public health, and the topics of the day.

_list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article presents a discussion format where security expert Juliette Kayyem and public health physician Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux address questions on security, public health, and current events, framed under the title 'COVID and the Coup'. While the provided body text lacks the detailed content of their discussion, it situates their dialogue within the broader context of the COVID-19 pandemic and its intersection with national security and governance challenges. The piece is part of a series featuring expert analyses on pressing global issues, linking to related content on topics such as mass migration, school reopenings, and technological applications in security and trade. The publication emphasizes the interconnectedness of public health crises, political stability, and security policy.
result.main_content_zh: 本文采用对话形式，由安全专家朱丽叶·凯耶姆和公共卫生医师玛格丽特·布尔多博士就安全、公共卫生和时事问题回答问题，主题为“COVID与政变”。虽然提供的正文文本未包含讨论的具体细节，但将他们的对话置于新冠疫情及其与国家安全和治理挑战相交织的更广泛背景中。这篇文章是系列专家分析的一部分，探讨紧迫的全球性问题，并链接到有关大规模移民、学校重新开放以及技术在安全和贸易中应用等相关内容。该出版物强调了公共卫生危机、政治稳定和安全政策之间的相互关联性。
